repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem:
2d795face6e4 added support for tab-local options ('cmdheight') to `nvim_get_option_value`, but not to:

    nvim_get_option_info2()
    nvim_set_option_value(…, { tab = … })
    gettabwinvar()


## Solution:
- Update `options.lua` to model tab-local options. Introduce `kOptScopeTab`.
- Handle tab scope in the options layer so it works for all options APIs.

## Note:
- `gettabvar()` not changed in this PR. Not sure if it should/need be.


fix https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/31140

cc @famiu

comment_data.body: Worth checking if there are assumptions anywhere that `!option_is_global_local(opt_idx)` implies that the option only has one scope flag set. I think it's unlikely, still worth taking a look.
Nit: might be cleaner to just use a switch-case at this point. Also scales better if we add/remove scopes since unhandled scopes would give an error (although adding more scopes is unlikely).
Wonder if this special case can eventually be removed by expanding what context is stored. Would an equivalent to `aco_save_T` or `switchwin_T` for tabs potentially  solve this?
I assume so but presumably isn't worth the blast-radius until (if ever) there are more tab-local options

title: feat(api): tab-local option scope
